channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The business of storage units is booming. We met Vermont's operators
item.description: Self-storage operators in Vermont report long waitlists and big profits. The state's tight housing market and rapidly aging population contribute to the growth.
item.content:encoded: Self-storage operators in Vermont report long waitlists and big profits. The state's tight housing market and rapidly aging population contribute to the growth.
item.dc:creator: Mikaela Lefrak

item.title: A bill proposes to increase the proportion of ethanol in gas to lower prices
item.description: Would adding more ethanol to gasoline lower prices at the pump? NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Bloomberg reporter Elizabeth Elkin about a bill that would allow 15% ethanol blends year-round.
item.content:encoded: Would adding more ethanol to gasoline lower prices at the pump? NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Bloomberg reporter Elizabeth Elkin about a bill that would allow 15% ethanol blends year-round.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: How a CEO and Trump donor is weaponizing tariffs against his rivals
item.description: Cambria CEO Marty Davis has successfully asked the U.S. government to put tariffs on quartz. His business competitors are crying foul.
item.content:encoded:
Cambria CEO Marty Davis has successfully asked the U.S. government to put tariffs on quartz. His business competitors are crying foul.

(Image credit: Richard Tsong-Taatarii/Star Tribune via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: New York Magazine reviewing work of writer facing plagiarism allegations
item.description: The high-profile writer, who has written a forthcoming book about New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani, is being investigated by the magazine after being called out for copying the work of other writers.
item.content:encoded:
The high-profile writer, who has written a forthcoming book about New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani, is being investigated by the magazine after being called out for copying the work of other writers.

(Image credit: YouTube)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Can the NFL's Rooney Rule survive the DEI backlash?
item.description: Florida's attorney general says the NFL's Rooney Rule, which requires teams to interview minority candidates for top jobs, is discriminatory. Trump's EEOC has challenged such policies elsewhere.
item.content:encoded:
Florida's attorney general says the NFL's Rooney Rule, which requires teams to interview minority candidates for top jobs, is discriminatory. Trump's EEOC has challenged such policies elsewhere.

(Image credit: Kevin C. Cox)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Gen Z homeowners? Yes, more in their 20s are managing to buy despite the odds
item.description: Gen Z homeowners now outpace millennials at the same age. They're more likely to be single and less likely to use help from parents.
item.content:encoded:
Gen Z homeowners now outpace millennials at the same age. They're more likely to be single and less likely to use help from parents.

(Image credit: Caleb Alvarado for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: From proposal to print: How books make it to store shelves
item.description: Our Planet Money team has gotten an inside peek at the publishing industry as they follow the journey of their book. They explain how economic forces shape what choices a bookstore makes.
item.content:encoded: Our Planet Money team has gotten an inside peek at the publishing industry as they follow the journey of their book. They explain how economic forces shape what choices a bookstore makes.
item.dc:creator: Alexi Horowitz-Ghazi

item.title: Here's how the NYT crafts bestseller lists — and how authors try to game them
item.description: We go inside how The New York Times crafts the powerful bestseller lists, and the history of ways authors try to game the system — and sometimes succeed.
item.content:encoded: We go inside how The New York Times crafts the powerful bestseller lists, and the history of ways authors try to game the system — and sometimes succeed.
item.dc:creator: Alexi Horowitz-Ghazi

item.title: How AI is speeding new business creation, especially among Gen Z entrepreneurs
item.description: Entrepreneurs are turning to AI to speed the creation of new businesses, with Gen Z leading the way. That's according to a new report from the payroll software firm Gusto.
item.content:encoded: Entrepreneurs are turning to AI to speed the creation of new businesses, with Gen Z leading the way. That's according to a new report from the payroll software firm Gusto.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Jury orders Boeing to pay $49.5 million to family of 737 MAX crash victim
item.description: A federal jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million to the family of Samya Stumo, a young woman who was killed in the second of two Boeing 737 MAX crashes within months of each other in 2018 and 2019.
item.content:encoded:
A federal jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million to the family of Samya Stumo, a young woman who was killed in the second of two Boeing 737 MAX crashes within months of each other in 2018 and 2019.

(Image credit: Stumo Family)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose
